user_message: Before my call with Northwind Traders, assess the risk. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 12:17 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 11:30 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Northwind Traders escalation triage

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 16:55 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Kevin (Northwind Traders)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Kevin (Northwind Traders): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:24:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-C trap (Generates generic talking points); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-C failure mode (Generates generic talking points)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-C -- Generates generic talking points
